title: Iliad, Book 5: The Aristeia of Diomedes
description: Book 5 of Homer's Iliad: The Aristeia of Diomedes.
introduction: Book 5 of the Iliad: The Aristeia of Diomedes. The Greek text is from the Venetus A manuscript (10th century), the oldest complete manuscript of the Iliad.

translation.original_text: Ἔνθ᾿ αὖ Τυδεΐδῃ Διομήδεϊ Παλλὰς Ἀθήνη δῶκε μένος καὶ θάρσος.
translation.text: Then Pallas Athena gave Diomedes, son of Tydeus, strength and courage, that he might be distinguished among all the Argives. She set unwearying fire blazing from his shield and helmet. Diomedes raged across the battlefield, wounding even the gods — he struck Aphrodite and drove his spear against Ares himself.
